LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR RELIEF AS A RESULT OF (1) FAILURE OF NUMEROUS THIRD PARTIES TO COMPLY WITH COURT'S OCTOBER 26, 2017 ORDER DIRECTING THEM TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS IN RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S SUBPOENAS DUCES TECUM BY NOVEMBER 9, 2017 AFTER DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH WAS DENIED; (2) DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO PRODUCE KEY DOCUMENTS AT HIS DEPOSITION; AND (3) REQUEST TO CONTINUE THE DECEMBER 11, 2017 TRIAL TO APRIL 9, 2017 OR THE FIRST AVAILABLE DATE ON THE COURT'S CALENDAR THEREAFTER. THIS MATTER IS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF SWEDELSONGOTTLIEB'S APPLICATION FOR A TRIAL CONTINUANCE TO APRIL 9, 2018 IS GRANTED. ALL DISCOVERY AND EXPERT DATES WILL BE TRIGGERED OFF THE NEW TRIAL DATE. THERE WILL BE NO FURTHER CONTINUANCES OF THE TRIAL ABSENT AN UNANTICIPATED EMERGENCY OR STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES. ON ITS OWN MOTION THE COURT HAS DETERMINED THAT A DISCOVERY REFEREE IS REQUIRED TO RESOLVE ALL PENDING AND FUTURE DISCOVERY DISPUTES. THE PARTIES ARE REQUIRED TO MEET AND CONFER IN AN EFFORT TO AGREE ON A DISCOVERY REFEREE. IN THE EVENT THAT THE PARTIES ARE UNABLE TO REACH AGREEMENT, THE COURT WILL APPOINT EVERETT HEWLETT, JR., A FORMER DISCOVERY COMMISSIONER OF THIS COURT, WHO IS CURRENTLY AFFILIATED WITH ADR SERVICES, INC. THE PARTIES ARE FURTHER REQUIRED TO MEET AND CONFER NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 11, 2017 REGARDING ALL PENDING AND ANTICIPATED DISCOVERY DISPUTES AND TO ADVISE THE DISCOVERY REFEREE ABOUT ALL SUCH DISPUTES NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 13, 2017. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Off Calendar Dec-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR RELIEF AS A RESULT OF (1) FAILURE OF NUMEROUS THIRD PARTIES TO COMPLY WITH COURT'S OCTOBER 26, 2017 ORDER DIRECTING THEM TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS IN RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S SUBPOENAS DUCES TECUM BY NOVEMBER 9, 2017 AFTER DEFT'S MOTION TO QUASH WAS DENIED (2) DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO PRODUCE KEY DOCUMENTS AT HIS DEPOSITION; AND (3) REQUEST TO CONTINUE THE DECEMBER 11, 2017 TRIAL TO APRIL 9, 2017 OR THE FIRST AVAILABLE DATE ON THE COURT'S CALENDAR THEREAFTER.

Appearances: Minh Hoang of SwedelsonGottlieb (310.659.6771) for plaintiff and moving party; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff SwedelsonGottlieb's application for a trial continuance to April 9, 2018 is granted. All discovery and expert dates will be triggered off the new trial date. There will be no further continuances of the trial absent an unanticipated emergency or stipulation of the parties. SG has shown that there is an array of significant discovery disputes that need to be addressed, it is likely entitled to receive substantial additional documents from the HOAs and defendant William Noland, and it reasonably needs to propound further discovery including notices for the depositions of the HOAs, all of which supports a trial continuance. Mr. Noland has not shown that he would suffer legal prejudice by a trial continuance. On its own motion the court has determined that a discovery referee is required to resolve all pending and future discovery disputes. The parties are required to meet and confer in an effort to agree on a discovery referee. If the parties desire, the court is willing to continue the hearing on this motion to give the parties more time to reach agreement on a discovery referee. In the event that the parties are unable to reach agreement, the court will appoint Everett Hewlett, Jr., a former discovery commissioner of this court, who is currently affiliated with ADR Services, Inc. The parties are further required to meet and confer no later than December 11, 2017 regarding all pending and anticipated discovery disputes and to advise the discovery referee about all such disputes no later than December 13, 2017. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR 5525 (email: vgross@sftc.org) (302/HEK)

Off Calendar Oct-26-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Chandler Bartlett, Esq. (310-659-6771) of Law Offices of Rodney T. Lewin for Plaintiff through Court Call.
John Anderson, Esq. (916-501-7251) for Defendant through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court hears all 3 motions simultaneously. Both sides Argued (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant William Alexander Noland'S Motion For Protective Order Quashing Plaintiff's August 10, 2017 Subpoenas Duces Tecum

Defendant William Noland's motion for protective order quashing plaintiff's August 10, 2017 subpoenas duces tecum is denied. All of the documents sought in each of the subpoenas must be produced to the depositon officer no later than November 9, 2017. Neither Mr. Noland nor any of the deponents have shown that production of the requested documents is unduly burdensome or that plainitff SwedelsonGottlieb should be required to conduct oral depositions of the deponents prior to receiving the documents requested. Except possibly subcategories 8(c) and (d), none of ten categories of documents sought necessarily call for documents protected by the attorney-client privilege, the work product doctrine or the right to privacy since none of the categories seek any documents reflecting a request for legal advice from Mr. Noland, the giving of such advice by Noland, any work product of Mr. Noland, or the private information of the deponents. If desired by a deponent, documents containing responsive information may be redacted to exclude information other than the information sought as long as the date of a document, the maker of a document and all persons receiving the document are not redacted. A deponent may, if it desires, designate some or all of the produced documents subject to the confidentiality protective order, but it may not invoke any designation that precludes either Ms. Gottlieb, Mr. Swedelson or SwedelsonGottlieb's's retained experts from reviewing those documents. As to subcategories 8(c) and (d), if any document containing any responsive information is withheld on grounds of privilege, work product or privacy, as to each such document the deponent must identify the document in a log and provide sufficient information about the document so that a prima facie case for assertion of the grounds for withholding the document is established and SwedelsonGottlieb and the court are able to evaluate the merits of the asserted grounds for withholding the document. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
